Sarandë's own local hospital (Spitali Sarandë) handles routine emergency/general/minor-surgery care

fact_label: Private hospital capability — American Hospital Tirana (Spitali Amerikan)

fact_label: Public hospital capability — University Medical Center of Tirana "Mother Teresa"

notes: Wikipedia, myhospitalnow.com, checked 2026-07-11

fact_label: Tirana mayor's detention — local municipal effect
notes: The arrest/detention itself is High-confidence, well-corroborated; any operational-effect claim on day-to-day municipal services/permitting/bureaucracy is Speculative and not made — the same national criterion the mayor's case is already scored under (not double-counted locally)

fact_label: Where the medevac line sits — Italy (Bari, Brindisi), Greece (Corfu, Athens)
value_raw: Most complex/specialized surgical cases only (cardiac and oncology edge cases named specifically) — occasionally referred onward; routine and many specialized/serious cases are handled locally
notes: medicaltourismal.com, checked 2026-07-11 — the materially higher local ceiling vs. Sarandë's "essentially any serious emergency" referral threshold

Off-limits to direct foreign-individual ownership — the real restriction sits on land, not buildings; a workaround structure (Albanian company, or buying only the building on registered residential land) is typically necessary

value_raw: Normal, unrestricted, sold/consumed openly nationwide despite a ~57% Muslim-background population; legal drinking age 18
notes: Raki (grape/fruit brandy, ~40% ABV) is the real national social lubricant. Real hard line: driving BAC limit is 0.01% — effectively zero tolerance

value_raw: Illegal recreationally; possession beyond a small amount, cultivation, sale, or distribution can carry 5-10 years imprisonment
notes: Law 61/2023 legalized medical/industrial cultivation for export only — no domestic retail/prescription access exists. Small-quantity possession may be charged administratively (fine ALL 10,000-50,000) as prosecutorial discretion, not a written decriminalization carve-out

value_raw: Properties within 200m of shoreline may fall under land-use restrictions even when marketed as an ordinary villa/apartment
notes: A real, specific caution for Sarandë given its coastal character — the same "check which side of the legal line a specific parcel sits on" pattern as Guatemala's 200m lakeshore band

value_raw: 'Persistent political polarization, mistrust and political control over electoral administration,' a possible shift toward broader 'crisis of the political system' in 2026
notes: A live, developing situation, not a settled one either way

source_ref: [GAP — named as existing, not run to full mechanical depth this pass]
notes: Implicitly covered by the Unique Permit's "work" half, but labor-market-test/sponsorship mechanics not chased

value_raw: All 6 negotiating clusters/33 chapters opened, none closed; EU's own tracking doesn't foresee closing judiciary/fundamental-rights clusters given current conditions
notes: Candidate since 2014, negotiations ongoing. An EU official has floated 2027 for completed negotiations — an aspiration, not a locked timeline. Not a near-term planning consideration for anyone arriving in the next 3+ years

fact_label: EU institutional read on rule of law
value_raw: European Parliament report + EPP statement (Feb 2026) named ongoing judicial pressure and persistent corruption as active concerns
notes: OSW's own April 2026 analysis titles its read "Rule of law concerns in Albania: an obstacle to EU accession" — a named current obstacle, not resolved

fact_label: Foreigner-targeted crime schemes (express kidnapping, ATM-cloning, etc.)

notes: A genuine open gap, not a confirmed absence
Ookla Speedtest Global Index, ranked 77th globally (upload ranked 78th, notably weaker). Mobile internet comparatively strong: 128.52 Mbps average, 37th globally

fact_label: Long tourist stay converts to residency
notes: The 365-day US-citizen stay does NOT convert to anything longer-term — the Unique Permit system must be filed separately, same structural shape as Guatemala's/Mexico's tourist clocks

source_ref: [GAP — not checked this pass at all]
notes: Every other active candidate in this project has this route documented; flagged so Albania's file doesn't read thinner by accident

Instability reads as chronic institutional friction (stalled accession, corruption prosecutions, judicial-pressure concerns) rather than one dated shock event — a real contrast with Bulgaria's own 2026 political churn

fact_label: Pet import (dog and cat) — rabies titer / quarantine requirement
value_raw: No mandatory quarantine if requirements met; microchip first, THEN rabies vaccination 21+ days before entry (sequencing matters); no titer test required from any country
notes: A seventh genuine exception to this project's dog-only pet-import gap (both species checked, identical requirements found). Pets from a country without equivalent regulations can be subject to quarantine — a real risk for non-EU/non-Annex-II origins. Up to 5 pets/traveler

fact_label: Private health insurance cost — local Albanian plans
notes: Basic-to-comprehensive range, same source as the age-ceiling row above

fact_label: Private health insurance enrollment age ceiling
value_raw: 60 (standard/silver/gold tiers); 79 (travel-oriented 'Live and Travel in Albania' package)
notes: One insurer's plan structure (SIGAL UNIQA Group, via the Expats in Albania group-rate program) — not independently cross-checked against a second Albanian insurer this pass. A real, lower-than-generically-assumed ceiling: this project's own working assumption going into this research pass was "commonly 65-75," and Albania's clearest sourced example undercuts that on the standard tiers

value_raw: Same title-deed rights as Albanian citizens, no reciprocity requirement
notes: Law No. 7850 — closer to Belize's/Morocco's clean freehold picture than Guatemala's/Mexico's restricted-zone complexity
Feeds the property-owner Unique Permit category, which must still be filed and granted separately

fact_label: Public healthcare system access for foreigners
value_raw: Gated on local employment / social-insurance contribution, not residency alone — a non-employed foreign resident (retiree, remote-worker, passive-income) reads as functionally private-only for planned/non-emergency care
notes: Real, load-bearing for a common non-employed foreign-resident profile — most plausible entry paths (Unique Permit's retiree/remote-worker/property-owner categories) land on the private-only side of this gate. Emergency care itself is a partial exception (see Sarandë's free-at-point-of-service row below), but doesn't extend to ongoing/planned care

value_raw: November 2019 Durrës earthquake (M6.4) killed 51, significant coastal damage — recent memory, not ancient history
notes: Not independently deep-dived with Guatemala's-Fuego-level rigor this pass; current building-code enforcement quality and Sarandë/Tirana-specific hazard mapping unconfirmed

fact_label: Tirana mayor Erion Veliaj — corruption prosecution
value_raw: Arrested by SPAK on corruption/money-laundering charges, ~110M lek (~$1.15M) in illicit income alleged; Constitutional Court rejected appeal, remains in pre-trial detention
notes: A national anti-corruption institution (SPAK) successfully prosecuting a sitting capital-city mayor — genuinely mixed as a signal: real accountability happening, against a backdrop of broader EU-named rule-of-law concern

fact_label: Tourist/visitor stay allowance — US citizens specifically
notes: A genuine, documented outlier — no residence permit needed; after the year, must leave and stay outside 90+ days before another visa-exempt period. Independently re-derived and confirmed here
The Schengen-style formula, even though Albania is not itself in Schengen

fact_label: Unique Permit — health-insurance proof as a hard document requirement
source_ref: [GAP — not confirmed against a primary Albanian legal text this pass]
notes: Several other candidates in this project gate their visa routes explicitly on proof of coverage — whether Albania's Unique Permit does the same, and whether the age-ceiling above would then compound with an income threshold, is a real open question, not yet confirmed either way

value_raw: Registered Albanian property ownership certificate, valued at a sufficient level (exact threshold unconfirmed)
notes: Issued 1 year, renewed annually — shorter-cycle than the retiree/remote-worker routes. A real structural link between a cash-property-purchase plan and a legal-residency mechanism, genuinely distinctive among this project's routes

fact_label: Unique Permit — property-owner category converts to permanent residency
value_raw: Same 5-year continuous-residence clock as every other temporary category
notes: Does not itself grant permanent status faster — not independently confirmed against a primary legal text

fact_label: Unique Permit — remote-worker route accepts passive income as qualifying proof
notes: Income must come from outside Albania and outside the local labor market — same active-income shape as every other candidate's digital-nomad-style route

fact_label: Unique Permit — remote-worker route tax treatment
value_raw: 12-month income-tax exemption on qualifying foreign income
notes: Cost ~€100, processed online. Post-12-month tax treatment and the 183-day tax-residency trigger's applicability not confirmed

fact_label: Unique Permit — remote-worker/"digital nomad" income requirement
value_raw: $9,800/year widely-repeated but not primary-sourced; €1,500/month the more conservative real-world planning figure
notes: The underlying law states "sufficient income," not a fixed statutory threshold — a genuine, named sourcing gap, not smoothed to one number

fact_label: Unique Permit — retiree category accepts passive income as qualifying proof
notes: Foreign nationals receiving a home-country pension qualify

fact_label: Unique Permit (Leje Unike) — retiree category income threshold
notes: ~€967/month once converted — a notably lower annual floor than several other candidates in this project. Same family as Guatemala's Rentista visa

fact_label: Unique Permit permanent status / naturalization clock
notes: Onward citizenship timeline/requirements beyond this permanent-residence milestone not swept this pass
